Name: latency-aware_subspace_routing
Title: Latency-Aware Subspace Routing: Training-Free Inference Optimization via Spectral Stability
Short Hypothesis: By dynamically routing tokens through low-rank subspaces based on their spectral stability, we can enforce strict latency budgets while maintaining inference accuracy, leveraging PAC-Bayes bounds to ensure generalization stability under perturbation.
Related Work: Existing work on training-free inference optimization, such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation, focuses on precision allocation or noise filtering but does not explicitly address latency-aware routing through low-rank subspaces. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating spectral stability diagnostics with dynamic routing mechanisms, grounded in PAC-Bayes theory, to enforce strict latency budgets.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for latency-aware inference optimization that dynamically routes tokens through low-rank subspaces based on their spectral stability. By computing real-time spectral diagnostics using randomized SVD, we map tokens to subspaces that enforce strict latency budgets while maintaining inference accuracy. Our approach is grounded in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking spectral stability to generalization stability under perturbation. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, our method achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to existing baselines. This work bridges foundational optimization theory with practical, scalable inference, offering a reproducible, zero-training path to efficient latency-aware routing.
Experiments: Evaluate the framework on WILDS benchmarks to measure accuracy retention under distributional shifts.
Conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of spectral stability thresholds on latency and accuracy.
Compare throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers against existing baselines such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Geometric Signal Separation.
Measure hidden-state perturbation overhead and ensure it remains within strict bounds.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of the routing mechanism may vary across different model architectures.
Real-time spectral diagnostics could introduce computational overhead, potentially offsetting latency gains.
The framework may require calibration for specific hardware configurations to achieve optimal performance.
